title: Fix kanbus_clock header color NameError and comment header color parity
description: Runtime bug in demos/kanbus_clock.py: undefined header_fg/main_fg in _build_card_scene_from_sections, and comment_added path omits header_issue_type_color/header_status_color causing inconsistent header coloring.

comments.author: ryan.porter
comments.text: Started: verifying the two reported kanbus_clock bugs and preparing a targeted patch + tests.

comments.author: ryan.porter
comments.text: Patched demos/kanbus_clock.py: (1) fixed undefined header_fg/main_fg in _build_card_scene_from_sections by using defined header_color/main_fg variables, (2) comment_added now passes header_issue_type_color and header_status_color explicitly. Validation: pytest tests/test_kanbus_clock.py -q => 44 passed.

comments.author: ryan.porter
comments.text: Hotfix execution started: verifying _RuntimeFrameRenderer.render wiring, startup guard, tests, and live bring-up.

comments.author: ryan.porter
comments.text: Hotfix complete: restored _RuntimeFrameRenderer.render/_ensure_runtime_loaded on the runtime renderer, added ReactClockScene fast-fail guard, and verified with py_compile + pytest (60 passing). Live bring-up now renders and processes issue_created/comment_added/state_transition events.
